definition: The mapping from a data field to a visual channel in a Vega-Lite L1 chart — e.g. color: { field: 'category' } means encode category by hue, not #3b82f6. The encoding is semantic; the concrete palette, scale range, and animation are the presentation/theme plane, resolved separately. A field's measurement type (quantitative / temporal / ordinal / nominal) drives the default scale and the accessibility-derivable axis/legend labels.
usage: The core distinction in the Web Charts semantic/presentation split (#572). Because color/size are semantic, the same chart spec re-themes for free and an a11y description is derivable from the encoding + measurement types — the conformance suite scores accessibility as a first-class axis (#570 slice d).
